atomicity.root: write the complete family to a sibling .partial directory, fsync every file, verify the closed inventory, then perform one same-volume rename
atomicity.world: write each world to a sibling .partial directory, fsync/close every file, verify hashes and sizes, then rename
atomicity.directory_durability: parent-directory fsync follows each rename where the OS exposes directory descriptors; Windows uses flushed files plus same-volume atomic rename because directory fsync is unavailable
atomicity.existing_path_policy: refuse if either final or partial root already exists; no retry or replacement

physics.npz.purpose: engine-complete raw state and StepLedger sequence; independent reproducer input

online.json.purpose: production detector/tracker/tracer diagnostics, raw edges/events/gates and online class

shard_manifest.json.purpose: world identity, enrolled coordinates, terminal status, exact file SHA-256/bytes, array names/shapes/dtypes and measurement row counts
shard_manifest.json.independent_reproducer_access: only identity/status and physics.npz integrity fields

root_files.classification.json: online world classes, complete nine-class atlas, candidate regions and disposition; forbidden to the independent reproducer
root_files.root_manifest.json: binds Stage-B manifest hash, raw-schema hash, enrollment hash/bytes, classification hash/bytes, and every enrolled shard-manifest hash/bytes/status

independent_reproduction_inputs: each shard_manifest.json identity/status and physics.npz integrity fields
canonical_json: UTF-8, sort_keys=true, separators comma/colon, allow_nan=false, terminal LF
publication_completion: exactly every enrolled world has one unique terminal shard with status COMPLETE, NUMERICAL_INVALID or INSTRUMENTATION_INVALID; root inventory binds every shard manifest
scientific_eligibility: DEV_REGIME_CANDIDATE or DEV_FEASIBILITY_FAIL requires every enrolled shard status COMPLETE and all hashes, shapes, dtypes, steps and Stage-A invariants valid
